chr_diabetes_prevalence.short_description: Percentage of adults aged 18+ with diagnosed diabetes (age-adjusted).
chr_diabetes_prevalence.long_description: Percentage of adults aged 18 and above with diagnosed diabetes (age-adjusted), from the CDC Division of Diabetes Translation.
chr_diabetes_prevalence.statement: In {location}, {value}% of adults had diagnosed diabetes.

chr_adult_obesity.long_description: Percentage of the adult population (age 18 and older) that reports a body mass index (BMI) greater than or equal to 30 kg/m2 (age-adjusted), from the BRFSS.

chr_poor_or_fair_health.long_name: Adults Reporting Poor or Fair Health
chr_poor_or_fair_health.short_description: Percentage of adults reporting fair or poor health (age-adjusted).
chr_poor_or_fair_health.long_description: Percentage of adults reporting fair or poor health (age-adjusted), based on self-reported health status from the Behavioral Risk Factor Surveillance System (BRFSS).
chr_poor_or_fair_health.statement: In {location}, {value}% of adults reported fair or poor health.

chr_poor_physical_health_days.long_name: Average Physically Unhealthy Days per Month
chr_poor_physical_health_days.short_description: Average number of physically unhealthy days reported in past 30 days (age-adjusted).
chr_poor_physical_health_days.long_description: Average number of physically unhealthy days reported in past 30 days (age-adjusted), from the BRFSS.
chr_poor_physical_health_days.statement: In {location}, adults reported an average of {value} physically unhealthy days per month.

chr_poor_mental_health_days.long_name: Average Mentally Unhealthy Days per Month
chr_poor_mental_health_days.short_description: Average number of mentally unhealthy days reported in past 30 days (age-adjusted).
chr_poor_mental_health_days.long_description: Average number of mentally unhealthy days reported in past 30 days (age-adjusted), from the BRFSS.
chr_poor_mental_health_days.statement: In {location}, adults reported an average of {value} mentally unhealthy days per month.

chr_premature_death.short_description: Years of potential life lost before age 75 per 100,000 population.
chr_premature_death.long_description: Years of potential life lost before age 75 per 100,000 population (age-adjusted). This measure captures the burden of premature death by weighting deaths that occur at younger ages more heavily.
chr_premature_death.statement: In {location}, the premature death rate was {value} years of potential life lost per 100,000 population.

chr_premature_age_adjusted_mortality.short_description: Number of deaths among residents under age 75 per 100,000 population (age-adjusted).
chr_premature_age_adjusted_mortality.long_description: Number of deaths among residents under age 75 per 100,000 population (age-adjusted), from the National Center for Health Statistics (NCHS) Multiple Cause of Death files.
chr_premature_age_adjusted_mortality.statement: In {location}, the premature age-adjusted mortality rate was {value} per 100,000 population.

chr_life_expectancy.short_description: Average number of years people are expected to live.
chr_life_expectancy.long_description: Average number of years a person born in a given area is expected to live, based on age-specific death rates, from the National Center for Health Statistics and CDC WONDER.
chr_life_expectancy.statement: In {location}, life expectancy at birth was {value} years.

chr_preventable_hospital_stays.short_description: Rate of hospital stays for ambulatory-care sensitive conditions per 100,000 Medicare enrollees.
chr_preventable_hospital_stays.long_description: Discharge rate for ambulatory care-sensitive (preventable) conditions per 100,000 Medicare enrollees, from the Dartmouth Atlas of Health Care.
chr_preventable_hospital_stays.statement: In {location}, there were {value} preventable hospital stays per 100,000 Medicare enrollees.

chr.name: County Health Rankings & Roadmaps
chr.organization: University of Wisconsin Population Health Institute
chr.location: County Health Rankings & Roadmaps Annual Data
chr.description: The County Health Rankings & Roadmaps (CHR&R) program, a collaboration between the Robert Wood Johnson Foundation and the University of Wisconsin Population Health Institute, ranks the health of nearly all counties in the nation. The annual rankings measure vital health factors including high school graduation rates, obesity, smoking, unemployment, access to healthy foods, the quality of air and water, income inequality, and teen births that influence how long and how well we live. Data are released annually and cover all U.S. counties. Raw data are publicly available through the Zenodo repository (https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.18157681) and span release years 2010 through the present.
chr.restrictions: CC BY 4.0. Attribution required. Cite as: University of Wisconsin Population Health Institute. County Health Rankings & Roadmaps. https://www.countyhealthrankings.org.
